---
title: Example session notes
session_id: session-01
event_id: null
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Session objective {#session-objective}

By the end of the session, students can explain how course metadata, session files, generated data, and Quarto rendering fit together in a reusable teaching repository.
:::

## Learning objectives {#learning-objectives}

Students should be able to:

- identify the role of `course.yml` as the course metadata source;
- distinguish reusable infrastructure from course-specific content;
- locate slides, notes, exercises, generated data, and publication settings;
- explain how handbook schedule synchronization supports maintainable session overviews.

## Preparation {#preparation}

Before class, render or inspect the site locally and check that the landing page shows the session overview.

Review `course.yml`, `index.qmd`, `slides/session_01.qmd`, and `data/sessions.generated.yml`.

Prepare a short example of a change that belongs in metadata and a change that belongs in session content.

## Timing and topics {#timing-and-topics}

| Time | Topic | Instructor action | Student activity |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 0--10 min | Orientation | Open the landing page and explain the repository purpose. | Identify visible course elements. |
| 10--25 min | Metadata model | Walk through `course.yml` and generated session data. | Mark values that change for a new course. |
| 25--45 min | Material structure | Compare slides, notes, and exercises for one session. | Explain why each artifact is separate. |
| 45--60 min | In-class exercise | Facilitate repository-customization decisions. | Propose minimal changes for a new course. |
| 60--70 min | Wrap-up | Summarize conventions and next steps. | Ask remaining questions. |

## Instructor guidance {#instructor-guidance}

Emphasize that the template is not a course by itself.

It is a starting point for courses that need consistent publication, rendering, and maintenance conventions.

When students suggest duplicating values, redirect them to the metadata source of truth.

When students ask where to add course-specific material, point to the corresponding session file.

::: {.callout-tip}
### Optional content marker {#optional-content-marker}

If time permits, show how `make sync-events` updates generated schedule data without requiring remote data during Quarto rendering.
:::

## Exercise instructions {#exercise-instructions}

Ask students to inspect `course.yml` and answer the exercise questions in `exercises/session_01.qmd`.

Then ask them to classify each proposed change as infrastructure, metadata, generated data, or teaching content.

## Common errors and questions {#common-errors-and-questions}

| Issue | Likely cause | Response |
|---|---|---|
| The same title appears in several files. | Metadata was copied instead of referenced. | Keep structural metadata in `course.yml`. |
| A session appears without materials. | The session file path is missing or incorrect. | Check the session references in `course.yml`. |
| Schedule data look stale. | Generated data were not refreshed after a schedule change. | Run `make sync-events`. |
| A column layout creates extra slides. | A level-2 heading was used inside columns. | Use bold labels or lower-level headings inside columns. |

## Fallback options {#fallback-options}

If Quarto is unavailable, inspect source files directly and explain the rendering flow conceptually.

If schedule synchronization is unavailable, use the configured sessions in `course.yml` and regenerate later.

## Lessons learned {#lessons-learned}

Record after teaching:

- Which repository conventions were clear?
- Which distinctions between metadata, generated data, and content were confusing?
- Which examples should be added, removed, or simplified next time?
